mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines signal monetary easing that reduces discount rates applied to equity valuations. A sustained 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within 20 trading days as market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 5.02% on 2007-08-01 suggests tightening cycle near peak
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day forward returns during FEDFUNDS decline periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity. When UNRATE increases by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 5 trading days as equity investors anticipate earnings compression.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6% on 2007-08-01 near cyclical lows
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite rising unemployment, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts decline signals weakening residential investment and forward-looking demand contraction. When HOUST falls below its 6-month moving average, S&P 500 forward returns decline over 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward and anticipates credit stress.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1381.0 (vintage) / 1354.0 (revised) on 2007-07-01 shows revision risk and potential weakness
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or correlation between HOUST declines and subsequent SPX returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth reflects real economic capacity utilization. When INDPRO decelerates (month-over-month growth < 0.1%), S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 20 trading days as investors recognize slowing profit growth and reduced capital deployment opportunities.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 114.4254 (vintage) / 101.863 (revised) on 2007-08-01 shows substantial revision risk
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or mean 20-day returns during INDPRO deceleration periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales and employment growth jointly signal consumer demand strength. When both RSAFS growth and PAYEMS growth decelerate simultaneously (each < 0.2% month-over-month), S&P 500 forward returns decline over 20 trading days as the market recognizes demand-side weakness and earnings risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both grow < 0.2% month-over-month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 65% of instances where both RSAFS and PAYEMS decelerate, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary policy constraint. When CPIAUCSL accelerates above this threshold, the Federal Reserve maintains or raises FEDFUNDS, compressing equity valuations. S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 208.028 on 2007-07-01 implies ~2.7% YoY inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation pressure
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of high-inflation periods, or joint effect of CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS on returns is not significant (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth decelerates when labor demand weakens. When PAYEMS month-over-month growth falls below 0.15%, S&P 500 forward returns decline within 20 trading days as investors anticipate earnings pressure from reduced consumer income and spending.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 138037.0 (vintage) on 2007-08-01 near cyclical peak
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances despite PAYEMS deceleration, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly capture demand momentum. When both GDPC1 quarterly growth and HOUST month-over-month growth decelerate below their respective 2-year medians, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative within 60 trading days as the market reprices growth expectations downward.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and HOUST both fall below their 2-year median growth rates
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11523.8 (vintage) / 16713.314 (revised) on 2007-04-01 shows extreme revision risk
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 65% of instances where both GDPC1 and HOUST decelerate, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.
